title: alectryon coriaceus
intro: Alectryon coriaceus, also known as the Horse-shoe Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains various compounds with anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Alectryon coriaceus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against certain bacterial infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark is often used to make infusions, decoctions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
